narrative:
Martin Baturina is an attacking midfielder representing Croatia. His overall rating stands at 7.1/10, placing him in the top 5% of attacking midfields at this tournament. His rating range of 6.7–7.5 reflects a solid performance sample from 324 minutes played.

Baturina demonstrates clinical finishing at this World Cup, scoring 0.278 goals per 90 minutes from a low 0.034 expected goals per 90. He provides 1.111 key passes per 90 minutes, indicating creative intent. However, his 0.556 shots per 90 minutes at the World Cup suggests a limited direct goal threat from open play.

With 70% of his overall rating derived from strong club form, Baturina serves as an important attacking pivot for Croatia. Scouts should monitor if he can increase his expected goal and assist contributions from his current 0.034 xG and 0.084 xA per 90 minutes. Fans should watch for his continued ability to convert limited chances and provide key passes in upcoming matches.
